content.text: Let me explore both codebases to understand how Hermes skills work and how Moltis currently handles tools/skills.

input.prompt:
Thoroughly explore ~/code/hermes-agent to understand how skills work. I need to understand:

1. How skills are defined (file format, metadata, structure)
2. How skills are loaded and discovered
3. How skills are invoked/executed at runtime
4. The relationship between skills and tools
5. Look at a few concrete skill examples (e.g. from research/, creative/, productivity/ categories)
6. How the skill registry/loader works
7. Any skill configuration or manifest files

Focus on the skill system architecture. Look at directories like skills/, src/skills/, or similar. Check package.json or pyproject.toml for the project type. Be very thorough — check multiple directories and naming conventions.

1→---
     2→name: arxiv
     3→description: Search and retrieve academic papers from arXiv using their free REST API. No API key needed. Search by keyword, author, category, or ID. Combine with web_extract or the ocr-and-documents skill to read full paper content.
     4→version: 1.0.0
     5→author: Hermes Agent
     6→license: MIT
     7→metadata:
     8→  hermes:
     9→    tags: [Research, Arxiv, Papers, Academic, Science, API]
    10→    related_skills: [ocr-and-documents]
    11→---
    12→
    13→# arXiv Research
    14→
    15→Search and retrieve academic papers from arXiv via their free REST API. No API key, no dependencies — just curl.
    16→
    17→## Quick Reference
    18→
    19→| Action | Command |
    20→|--------|---------|
    21→| Search papers | `curl "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?search_query=all:QUERY&max_results=5"` |
    22→| Get specific paper | `curl "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?id_list=2402.03300"` |
    23→| Read abstract (web) | `web_extract(urls=["https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.03300"])` |
    24→| Read full paper (PDF) | `web_extract(urls=["https://arxiv.org/pdf/2402.03300"])` |
    25→
    26→## Searching Papers
    27→
    28→The API returns Atom XML. Parse with `grep`/`sed` or pipe through `python3` for clean output.
    29→
    30→### Basic search
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?search_query=all:GRPO+reinforcement+learning&max_results=5"
    34→```
    35→
    36→### Clean output (parse XML to readable format)
    37→
    38→```bash
    39→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?search_query=all:GRPO+reinforcement+learning&max_results=5&sortBy=submittedDate&sortOrder=descending" | python3 -c "
    40→import sys, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
    41→ns = {'a': 'http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom'}
    42→root = ET.parse(sys.stdin).getroot()
    43→for i, entry in enumerate(root.findall('a:entry', ns)):
    44→    title = entry.find('a:title', ns).text.strip().replace('\n', ' ')
    45→    arxiv_id = entry.find('a:id', ns).text.strip().split('/abs/')[-1]
    46→    published = entry.find('a:published', ns).text[:10]
    47→    authors = ', '.join(a.find('a:name', ns).text for a in entry.findall('a:author', ns))
    48→    summary = entry.find('a:summary', ns).text.strip()[:200]
    49→    cats = ', '.join(c.get('term') for c in entry.findall('a:category', ns))
    50→    print(f'{i+1}. [{arxiv_id}] {title}')
    51→    print(f'   Authors: {authors}')
    52→    print(f'   Published: {published} | Categories: {cats}')
    53→    print(f'   Abstract: {summary}...')
    54→    print(f'   PDF: https://arxiv.org/pdf/{arxiv_id}')
    55→    print()
    56→"
    57→```
    58→
    59→## Search Query Syntax
    60→
    61→| Prefix | Searches | Example |
    62→|--------|----------|---------|
    63→| `all:` | All fields | `all:transformer+attention` |
    64→| `ti:` | Title | `ti:large+language+models` |
    65→| `au:` | Author | `au:vaswani` |
    66→| `abs:` | Abstract | `abs:reinforcement+learning` |
    67→| `cat:` | Category | `cat:cs.AI` |
    68→| `co:` | Comment | `co:accepted+NeurIPS` |
    69→
    70→### Boolean operators
    71→
    72→```
    73→# AND (default when using +)
    74→search_query=all:transformer+attention
    75→
    76→# OR
    77→search_query=all:GPT+OR+all:BERT
    78→
    79→# AND NOT
    80→search_query=all:language+model+ANDNOT+all:vision
    81→
    82→# Exact phrase
    83→search_query=ti:"chain+of+thought"
    84→
    85→# Combined
    86→search_query=au:hinton+AND+cat:cs.LG
    87→```
    88→
    89→## Sort and Pagination
    90→
    91→| Parameter | Options |
    92→|-----------|---------|
    93→| `sortBy` | `relevance`, `lastUpdatedDate`, `submittedDate` |
    94→| `sortOrder` | `ascending`, `descending` |
    95→| `start` | Result offset (0-based) |
    96→| `max_results` | Number of results (default 10, max 30000) |
    97→
    98→```bash
    99→# Latest 10 papers in cs.AI
   100→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?search_query=cat:cs.AI&sortBy=submittedDate&sortOrder=descending&max_results=10"

100→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?search_query=cat:cs.AI&sortBy=submittedDate&sortOrder=descending&max_results=10"
   101→```
   102→
   103→## Fetching Specific Papers
   104→
   105→```bash
   106→# By arXiv ID
   107→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?id_list=2402.03300"
   108→
   109→# Multiple papers
   110→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?id_list=2402.03300,2401.12345,2403.00001"
   111→```
   112→
   113→## BibTeX Generation
   114→
   115→After fetching metadata for a paper, generate a BibTeX entry:
   116→
   117→{% raw %}
   118→```bash
   119→curl -s "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query?id_list=1706.03762" | python3 -c "
   120→import sys, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
   121→ns = {'a': 'http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom', 'arxiv': 'http://arxiv.org/schemas/atom'}
   122→root = ET.parse(sys.stdin).getroot()
   123→entry = root.find('a:entry', ns)
   124→if entry is None: sys.exit('Paper not found')
   125→title = entry.find('a:title', ns).text.strip().replace('\n', ' ')
   126→authors = ' and '.join(a.find('a:name', ns).text for a in entry.findall('a:author', ns))
   127→year = entry.find('a:published', ns).text[:4]
   128→raw_id = entry.find('a:id', ns).text.strip().split('/abs/')[-1]
   129→cat = entry.find('arxiv:primary_category', ns)
   130→primary = cat.get('term') if cat is not None else 'cs.LG'
   131→last_name = entry.find('a:author', ns).find('a:name', ns).text.split()[-1]
   132→print(f'@article{{{last_name}{year}_{raw_id.replace(\".\", \"\")},')
   133→print(f'  title     = {{{title}}},')
   134→print(f'  author    = {{{authors}}},')
   135→print(f'  year      = {{{year}}},')
   136→print(f'  eprint    = {{{raw_id}}},')
   137→print(f'  archivePrefix = {{arXiv}},')
   138→print(f'  primaryClass  = {{{primary}}},')
   139→print(f'  url       = {{https://arxiv.org/abs/{raw_id}}}')
   140→print('}')
   141→"
   142→```
   143→{% endraw %}
   144→
   145→## Reading Paper Content
   146→
   147→After finding a paper, read it:
   148→

1→"""Lightweight skill metadata utilities shared by prompt_builder and skills_tool.
     2→
     3→This module intentionally avoids importing the tool registry, CLI config, or any
     4→heavy dependency chain.  It is safe to import at module level without triggering
     5→tool registration or provider resolution.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import logging
     9→import os
    10→import re
    11→import sys
    12→from pathlib import Path
    13→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple
    14→
    15→from hermes_constants import get_config_path, get_skills_dir
    16→
    17→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    18→
    19→# ── Platform mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→PLATFORM_MAP = {
    22→    "macos": "darwin",
    23→    "linux": "linux",
    24→    "windows": "win32",
    25→}
    26→
    27→EXCLUDED_SKILL_DIRS = frozenset((".git", ".github", ".hub"))
    28→
    29→# ── Lazy YAML loader ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30→
    31→_yaml_load_fn = None
    32→
    33→
    34→def yaml_load(content: str):
    35→    """Parse YAML with lazy import and CSafeLoader preference."""
    36→    global _yaml_load_fn
    37→    if _yaml_load_fn is None:
    38→        import yaml
    39→
    40→        loader = getattr(yaml, "CSafeLoader", None) or yaml.SafeLoader
    41→
    42→        def _load(value: str):
    43→            return yaml.load(value, Loader=loader)
    44→
    45→        _yaml_load_fn = _load
    46→    return _yaml_load_fn(content)
    47→
    48→
    49→# ── Frontmatter parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50→
    51→
    52→def parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], str]:
    53→    """Parse YAML frontmatter from a markdown string.
    54→
    55→    Uses yaml with CSafeLoader for full YAML support (nested metadata, lists)
    56→    with a fallback to simple key:value splitting for robustness.
    57→
    58→    Returns:
    59→        (frontmatter_dict, remaining_body)
    60→    """
    61→    frontmatter: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    62→    body = content
    63→
    64→    if not content.startswith("---"):
    65→        return frontmatter, body
    66→
    67→    end_match = re.search(r"\n---\s*\n", content[3:])
    68→    if not end_match:
    69→        return frontmatter, body
    70→
    71→    yaml_content = content[3 : end_match.start() + 3]
    72→    body = content[end_match.end() + 3 :]
    73→
    74→    try:
    75→        parsed = yaml_load(yaml_content)
    76→        if isinstance(parsed, dict):
    77→            frontmatter = parsed
    78→    except Exception:
    79→        # Fallback: simple key:value parsing for malformed YAML
    80→        for line in yaml_content.strip().split("\n"):
    81→            if ":" not in line:
    82→                continue
    83→            key, value = line.split(":", 1)
    84→            frontmatter[key.strip()] = value.strip()
    85→
    86→    return frontmatter, body
    87→
    88→
    89→# ── Platform matching ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    90→
    91→
    92→def skill_matches_platform(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    93→    """Return True when the skill is compatible with the current OS.
    94→
    95→    Skills declare platform requirements via a top-level ``platforms`` list
    96→    in their YAML frontmatter::
    97→
    98→        platforms: [macos]          # macOS only
    99→        platforms: [macos, linux]   # macOS and Linux
   100→
   101→    If the field is absent or empty the skill is compatible with **all**
   102→    platforms (backward-compatible default).
   103→    """
   104→    platforms = frontmatter.get("platforms")
   105→    if not platforms:
   106→        return True
   107→    if not isinstance(platforms, list):
   108→        platforms = [platforms]
   109→    current = sys.platform
   110→    for platform in platforms:
   111→        normalized = str(platform).lower().strip()
   112→        mapped = PLATFORM_MAP.get(normalized, normalized)
   113→        if current.startswith(mapped):
   114→            return True
   115→    return False
   116→
   117→
   118→# ── Disabled skills ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   119→
   120→
   121→def get_disabled_skill_names(platform: str | None = None) -> Set[str]:
   122→    """Read disabled skill names from config.yaml.
   123→
   124→    Args:
   125→        platform: Explicit platform name (e.g. ``"telegram"``).  When
   126→            *None*, resolves from ``HERMES_PLATFORM`` or
   127→            ``HERMES_SESSION_PLATFORM`` env vars.  Falls back to the
   128→            global disabled list when no platform is determined.
   129→
   130→    Reads the config file directly (no CLI config imports) to stay
   131→    lightweight.
   132→    """
   133→    config_path = get_config_path()
   134→    if not config_path.exists():
   135→        return set()
   136→    try:
   137→        parsed = yaml_load(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   138→    except Exception as e:
   139→        logger.debug("Could not read skill config %s: %s", config_path, e)
   140→        return set()
   141→    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
   142→        return set()
   143→
   144→    skills_cfg = parsed.get("skills")
   145→    if not isinstance(skills_cfg, dict):
   146→        return set()
   147→
   148→    from gateway.session_context import get_session_env
   149→    resolved_platform = (
   150→        platform
   151→        or os.getenv("HERMES_PLATFORM")
   152→        or get_session_env("HERMES_SESSION_PLATFORM")
   153→    )
   154→    if resolved_platform:
   155→        platform_disabled = (skills_cfg.get("platform_disabled") or {}).get(
   156→            resolved_platform
   157→        )
   158→        if platform_disabled is not None:
   159→            return _normalize_string_set(platform_disabled)
   160→    return _normalize_string_set(skills_cfg.get("disabled"))
   161→
   162→
   163→def _normalize_string_set(values) -> Set[str]:
   164→    if values is None:
   165→        return set()
   166→    if isinstance(values, str):
   167→        values = [values]
   168→    return {str(v).strip() for v in values if str(v).strip()}
   169→
   170→
   171→# ── External skills directories ──────────────────────────────────────────
   172→
   173→
   174→def get_external_skills_dirs() -> List[Path]:
   175→    """Read ``skills.external_dirs`` from config.yaml and return validated paths.
   176→
   177→    Each entry is expanded (``~`` and ``${VAR}``) and resolved to an absolute
   178→    path.  Only directories that actually exist are returned.  Duplicates and
   179→    paths that resolve to the local ``~/.hermes/skills/`` are silently skipped.
   180→    """
   181→    config_path = get_config_path()
   182→    if not config_path.exists():
   183→        return []
   184→    try:
   185→        parsed = yaml_load(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   186→    except Exception:
   187→        return []
   188→    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
   189→        return []
   190→
   191→    skills_cfg = parsed.get("skills")
   192→    if not isinstance(skills_cfg, dict):
   193→        return []
   194→
   195→    raw_dirs = skills_cfg.get("external_dirs")
   196→    if not raw_dirs:
   197→        return []
   198→    if isinstance(raw_dirs, str):
   199→        raw_dirs = [raw_dirs]
   200→    if not isinstance(raw_dirs, list):
   201→        return []
   202→
   203→    local_skills = get_skills_dir().resolve()
   204→    seen: Set[Path] = set()
   205→    result: List[Path] = []
   206→
   207→    for entry in raw_dirs:
   208→        entry = str(entry).strip()
   209→        if not entry:
   210→            continue
   211→        # Expand ~ and environment variables
   212→        expanded = os.path.expanduser(os.path.expandvars(entry))
   213→        p = Path(expanded).resolve()
   214→        if p == local_skills:
   215→            continue
   216→        if p in seen:
   217→            continue
   218→        if p.is_dir():
   219→            seen.add(p)
   220→            result.append(p)
   221→        else:
   222→            logger.debug("External skills dir does not exist, skipping: %s", p)
   223→
   224→    return result
   225→
   226→
   227→def get_all_skills_dirs() -> List[Path]:
   228→    """Return all skill directories: local ``~/.hermes/skills/`` first, then external.
   229→
   230→    The local dir is always first (and always included even if it doesn't exist
   231→    yet — callers handle that).  External dirs follow in config order.
   232→    """
   233→    dirs = [get_skills_dir()]
   234→    dirs.extend(get_external_skills_dirs())
   235→    return dirs
   236→
   237→
   238→# ── Condition extraction ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   239→
   240→
   241→def extract_skill_conditions(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, List]:
   242→    """Extract conditional activation fields from parsed frontmatter."""
   243→    metadata = frontmatter.get("metadata")
   244→    # Handle cases where metadata is not a dict (e.g., a string from malformed YAML)
   245→    if not isinstance(metadata, dict):
   246→        metadata = {}
   247→    hermes = metadata.get("hermes") or {}
   248→    if not isinstance(hermes, dict):
   249→        hermes = {}
   250→    return {
   251→        "fallback_for_toolsets": hermes.get("fallback_for_toolsets", []),
   252→        "requires_toolsets": hermes.get("requires_toolsets", []),
   253→        "fallback_for_tools": hermes.get("fallback_for_tools", []),
   254→        "requires_tools": hermes.get("requires_tools", []),
   255→    }
   256→
   257→
   258→# ── Skill config extraction ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   259→
   260→
   261→def extract_skill_config_vars(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   262→    """Extract config variable declarations from parsed frontmatter.
   263→
   264→    Skills declare config.yaml settings they need via::
   265→
   266→        metadata:
   267→          hermes:
   268→            config:
   269→              - key: wiki.path
   270→                description: Path to the LLM Wiki knowledge base directory
   271→                default: "~/wiki"
   272→                prompt: Wiki directory path
   273→
   274→    Returns a list of dicts with keys: ``key``, ``description``, ``default``,
   275→    ``prompt``.  Invalid or incomplete entries are silently skipped.
   276→    """
   277→    metadata = frontmatter.get("metadata")
   278→    if not isinstance(metadata, dict):
   279→        return []
   280→    hermes = metadata.get("hermes")
   281→    if not isinstance(hermes, dict):
   282→        return []
   283→    raw = hermes.get("config")
   284→    if not raw:
   285→        return []
   286→    if isinstance(raw, dict):
   287→        raw = [raw]
   288→    if not isinstance(raw, list):
   289→        return []
   290→
   291→    result: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   292→    seen: set = set()
   293→    for item in raw:
   294→        if not isinstance(item, dict):
   295→            continue
   296→        key = str(item.get("key", "")).strip()
   297→        if not key or key in seen:
   298→            continue
   299→        # Must have at least key and description
   300→        desc = str(item.get("description", "")).strip()
   301→        if not desc:
   302→            continue
   303→        entry: Dict[str, Any] = {
   304→            "key": key,
   305→            "description": desc,
   306→        }
   307→        default = item.get("default")
   308→        if default is not None:
   309→            entry["default"] = default
   310→        prompt_text = item.get("prompt")
   311→        if isinstance(prompt_text, str) and prompt_text.strip():
   312→            entry["prompt"] = prompt_text.strip()
   313→        else:
   314→            entry["prompt"] = desc
   315→        seen.add(key)
   316→        result.append(entry)
   317→    return result
   318→
   319→
   320→def discover_all_skill_config_vars() -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   321→    """Scan all enabled skills and collect their config variable declarations.
   322→
   323→    Walks every skills directory, parses each SKILL.md frontmatter, and returns
   324→    a deduplicated list of config var dicts.  Each dict also includes a
   325→    ``skill`` key with the skill name for attribution.
   326→
   327→    Disabled and platform-incompatible skills are excluded.
   328→    """
   329→    all_vars: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   330→    seen_keys: set = set()
   331→
   332→    disabled = get_disabled_skill_names()
   333→    for skills_dir in get_all_skills_dirs():
   334→        if not skills_dir.is_dir():
   335→            continue
   336→        for skill_file in iter_skill_index_files(skills_dir, "SKILL.md"):
   337→            try:
   338→                raw = skill_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   339→                frontmatter, _ = parse_frontmatter(raw)
   340→            except Exception:
   341→                continue
   342→
   343→            skill_name = frontmatter.get("name") or skill_file.parent.name
   344→            if str(skill_name) in disabled:
   345→                continue
   346→            if not skill_matches_platform(frontmatter):
   347→                continue
   348→
   349→            config_vars = extract_skill_config_vars(frontmatter)
   350→            for var in config_vars:
   351→                if var["key"] not in seen_keys:
   352→                    var["skill"] = str(skill_name)
   353→                    all_vars.append(var)
   354→                    seen_keys.add(var["key"])
   355→
   356→    return all_vars
   357→
   358→
   359→# Storage prefix: all skill config vars are stored under skills.config.*
   360→# in config.yaml.  Skill authors declare logical keys (e.g. "wiki.path");
   361→# the system adds this prefix for storage and strips it for display.
   362→SKILL_CONFIG_PREFIX = "skills.config"
   363→
   364→
   365→def _resolve_dotpath(config: Dict[str, Any], dotted_key: str):
   366→    """Walk a nested dict following a dotted key.  Returns None if any part is missing."""
   367→    parts = dotted_key.split(".")
   368→    current = config
   369→    for part in parts:
   370→        if isinstance(current, dict) and part in current:
   371→            current = current[part]
   372→        else:
   373→            return None
   374→    return current
   375→
   376→
   377→def resolve_skill_config_values(
   378→    config_vars: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   379→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   380→    """Resolve current values for skill config vars from config.yaml.
   381→
   382→    Skill config is stored under ``skills.config.<key>`` in config.yaml.
   383→    Returns a dict mapping **logical** keys (as declared by skills) to their
   384→    current values (or the declared default if the key isn't set).
   385→    Path values are expanded via ``os.path.expanduser``.
   386→    """
   387→    config_path = get_config_path()
   388→    config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   389→    if config_path.exists():
   390→        try:
   391→            parsed = yaml_load(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   392→            if isinstance(parsed, dict):
   393→                config = parsed
   394→        except Exception:
   395→            pass
   396→
   397→    resolved: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   398→    for var in config_vars:
   399→        logical_key = var["key"]
   400→        storage_key = f"{SKILL_CONFIG_PREFIX}.{logical_key}"
   401→        value = _resolve_dotpath(config, storage_key)
   402→
   403→        if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and not value.strip()):
   404→            value = var.get("default", "")
   405→
   406→        # Expand ~ in path-like values
   407→        if isinstance(value, str) and ("~" in value or "${" in value):
   408→            value = os.path.expanduser(os.path.expandvars(value))
   409→
   410→        resolved[logical_key] = value
   411→
   412→    return resolved
   413→
   414→
   415→# ── Description extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   416→
   417→
   418→def extract_skill_description(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   419→    """Extract a truncated description from parsed frontmatter."""
   420→    raw_desc = frontmatter.get("description", "")
   421→    if not raw_desc:
   422→        return ""
   423→    desc = str(raw_desc).strip().strip("'\"")
   424→    if len(desc) > 60:
   425→        return desc[:57] + "..."
   426→    return desc
   427→
   428→
   429→# ── File iteration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   430→
   431→
   432→def iter_skill_index_files(skills_dir: Path, filename: str):
   433→    """Walk skills_dir yielding sorted paths matching *filename*.
   434→
   435→    Excludes ``.git``, ``.github``, ``.hub`` directories.
   436→    """
   437→    matches = []
   438→    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(skills_dir):
   439→        dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d not in EXCLUDED_SKILL_DIRS]
   440→        if filename in files:
   441→            matches.append(Path(root) / filename)
   442→    for path in sorted(matches, key=lambda p: str(p.relative_to(skills_dir))):
   443→        yield path
   444→
   445→
   446→# ── Namespace helpers for plugin-provided skills ───────────────────────────
   447→
   448→_NAMESPACE_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$")
   449→
   450→
   451→def parse_qualified_name(name: str) -> Tuple[Optional[str], str]:
   452→    """Split ``'namespace:skill-name'`` into ``(namespace, bare_name)``.
   453→
   454→    Returns ``(None, name)`` when there is no ``':'``.
   455→    """
   456→    if ":" not in name:
   457→        return None, name
   458→    return tuple(name.split(":", 1))  # type: ignore[return-value]
   459→
   460→
   461→def is_valid_namespace(candidate: Optional[str]) -> bool:
   462→    """Check whether *candidate* is a valid namespace (``[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+``)."""
   463→    if not candidate:
   464→        return False
   465→    return bool(_NAMESPACE_RE.match(candidate))
   466→

1→"""Shared slash command helpers for skills and built-in prompt-style modes.
     2→
     3→Shared between CLI (cli.py) and gateway (gateway/run.py) so both surfaces
     4→can invoke skills via /skill-name commands and prompt-only built-ins like
     5→/plan.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import json
     9→import logging
    10→import re
    11→from datetime import datetime
    12→from pathlib import Path
    13→from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    14→
    15→from hermes_constants import display_hermes_home
    16→
    17→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    18→
    19→_skill_commands: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
    20→_PLAN_SLUG_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9]+")
    21→# Patterns for sanitizing skill names into clean hyphen-separated slugs.
    22→_SKILL_INVALID_CHARS = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9-]")
    23→_SKILL_MULTI_HYPHEN = re.compile(r"-{2,}")
    24→
    25→
    26→def build_plan_path(
    27→    user_instruction: str = "",
    28→    *,
    29→    now: datetime | None = None,
    30→) -> Path:
    31→    """Return the default workspace-relative markdown path for a /plan invocation.
    32→
    33→    Relative paths are intentional: file tools are task/backend-aware and resolve
    34→    them against the active working directory for local, docker, ssh, modal,
    35→    daytona, and similar terminal backends. That keeps the plan with the active
    36→    workspace instead of the Hermes host's global home directory.
    37→    """
    38→    slug_source = (user_instruction or "").strip().splitlines()[0] if user_instruction else ""
    39→    slug = _PLAN_SLUG_RE.sub("-", slug_source.lower()).strip("-")
    40→    if slug:
    41→        slug = "-".join(part for part in slug.split("-")[:8] if part)[:48].strip("-")
    42→    slug = slug or "conversation-plan"
    43→    timestamp = (now or datetime.now()).strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S")
    44→    return Path(".hermes") / "plans" / f"{timestamp}-{slug}.md"
    45→
    46→
    47→def _load_skill_payload(skill_identifier: str, task_id: str | None = None) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], Path | None, str] | None:
    48→    """Load a skill by name/path and return (loaded_payload, skill_dir, display_name)."""
    49→    raw_identifier = (skill_identifier or "").strip()
    50→    if not raw_identifier:
    51→        return None
    52→
    53→    try:
    54→        from tools.skills_tool import SKILLS_DIR, skill_view
    55→
    56→        identifier_path = Path(raw_identifier).expanduser()
    57→        if identifier_path.is_absolute():
    58→            try:
    59→                normalized = str(identifier_path.resolve().relative_to(SKILLS_DIR.resolve()))
    60→            except Exception:
    61→                normalized = raw_identifier
    62→        else:
    63→            normalized = raw_identifier.lstrip("/")
    64→
    65→        loaded_skill = json.loads(skill_view(normalized, task_id=task_id))
    66→    except Exception:
    67→        return None
    68→
    69→    if not loaded_skill.get("success"):
    70→        return None
    71→
    72→    skill_name = str(loaded_skill.get("name") or normalized)
    73→    skill_path = str(loaded_skill.get("path") or "")
    74→    skill_dir = None
    75→    # Prefer the absolute skill_dir returned by skill_view() — this is
    76→    # correct for both local and external skills.  Fall back to the old
    77→    # SKILLS_DIR-relative reconstruction only when skill_dir is absent
    78→    # (e.g. legacy skill_view responses).
    79→    abs_skill_dir = loaded_skill.get("skill_dir")
    80→    if abs_skill_dir:
    81→        skill_dir = Path(abs_skill_dir)
    82→    elif skill_path:
    83→        try:
    84→            skill_dir = SKILLS_DIR / Path(skill_path).parent
    85→        except Exception:
    86→            skill_dir = None
    87→
    88→    return loaded_skill, skill_dir, skill_name
    89→
    90→
    91→def _inject_skill_config(loaded_skill: dict[str, Any], parts: list[str]) -> None:
    92→    """Resolve and inject skill-declared config values into the message parts.
    93→
    94→    If the loaded skill's frontmatter declares ``metadata.hermes.config``
    95→    entries, their current values (from config.yaml or defaults) are appended
    96→    as a ``[Skill config: ...]`` block so the agent knows the configured values
    97→    without needing to read config.yaml itself.
    98→    """
    99→    try:
   100→        from agent.skill_utils import (
   101→            extract_skill_config_vars,
   102→            parse_frontmatter,
   103→            resolve_skill_config_values,
   104→        )
   105→
   106→        # The loaded_skill dict contains the raw content which includes frontmatter
   107→        raw_content = str(loaded_skill.get("raw_content") or loaded_skill.get("content") or "")
   108→        if not raw_content:
   109→            return
   110→
   111→        frontmatter, _ = parse_frontmatter(raw_content)
   112→        config_vars = extract_skill_config_vars(frontmatter)
   113→        if not config_vars:
   114→            return
   115→
   116→        resolved = resolve_skill_config_values(config_vars)
   117→        if not resolved:
   118→            return
   119→
   120→        lines = ["", f"[Skill config (from {display_hermes_home()}/config.yaml):"]
   121→        for key, value in resolved.items():
   122→            display_val = str(value) if value else "(not set)"
   123→            lines.append(f"  {key} = {display_val}")
   124→        lines.append("]")
   125→        parts.extend(lines)
   126→    except Exception:
   127→        pass  # Non-critical — skill still loads without config injection
   128→
   129→
   130→def _build_skill_message(
   131→    loaded_skill: dict[str, Any],
   132→    skill_dir: Path | None,
   133→    activation_note: str,
   134→    user_instruction: str = "",
   135→    runtime_note: str = "",
   136→) -> str:
   137→    """Format a loaded skill into a user/system message payload."""
   138→    from tools.skills_tool import SKILLS_DIR
   139→
   140→    content = str(loaded_skill.get("content") or "")
   141→
   142→    parts = [activation_note, "", content.strip()]
   143→
   144→    # ── Inject resolved skill config values ──
   145→    _inject_skill_config(loaded_skill, parts)
   146→
   147→    if loaded_skill.get("setup_skipped"):
   148→        parts.extend(
   149→            [
   150→                "",

150→                "",
   151→                "[Skill setup note: Required environment setup was skipped. Continue loading the skill and explain any reduced functionality if it matters.]",
   152→            ]
   153→        )
   154→    elif loaded_skill.get("gateway_setup_hint"):
   155→        parts.extend(
   156→            [
   157→                "",
   158→                f"[Skill setup note: {loaded_skill['gateway_setup_hint']}]",
   159→            ]
   160→        )
   161→    elif loaded_skill.get("setup_needed") and loaded_skill.get("setup_note"):
   162→        parts.extend(
   163→            [
   164→                "",
   165→                f"[Skill setup note: {loaded_skill['setup_note']}]",
   166→            ]
   167→        )
   168→
   169→    supporting = []
   170→    linked_files = loaded_skill.get("linked_files") or {}
   171→    for entries in linked_files.values():
   172→        if isinstance(entries, list):
   173→            supporting.extend(entries)
   174→
   175→    if not supporting and skill_dir:
   176→        for subdir in ("references", "templates", "scripts", "assets"):
   177→            subdir_path = skill_dir / subdir
   178→            if subdir_path.exists():
   179→                for f in sorted(subdir_path.rglob("*")):
   180→                    if f.is_file() and not f.is_symlink():
   181→                        rel = str(f.relative_to(skill_dir))
   182→                        supporting.append(rel)
   183→
   184→    if supporting and skill_dir:
   185→        try:
   186→            skill_view_target = str(skill_dir.relative_to(SKILLS_DIR))
   187→        except ValueError:
   188→            # Skill is from an external dir — use the skill name instead
   189→            skill_view_target = skill_dir.name
   190→        parts.append("")
   191→        parts.append("[This skill has supporting files you can load with the skill_view tool:]")
   192→        for sf in supporting:
   193→            parts.append(f"- {sf}")
   194→        parts.append(
   195→            f'\nTo view any of these, use: skill_view(name="{skill_view_target}", file_path="<path>")'
   196→        )
   197→
   198→    if user_instruction:
   199→        parts.append("")
   200→        parts.append(f"The user has provided the following instruction alongside the skill invocation: {user_instruction}")
   201→
   202→    if runtime_note:
   203→        parts.append("")
   204→        parts.append(f"[Runtime note: {runtime_note}]")
   205→
   206→    return "\n".join(parts)
   207→
   208→
   209→def scan_skill_commands() -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
   210→    """Scan ~/.hermes/skills/ and return a mapping of /command -> skill info.
   211→
   212→    Returns:
   213→        Dict mapping "/skill-name" to {name, description, skill_md_path, skill_dir}.
   214→    """
   215→    global _skill_commands
   216→    _skill_commands = {}
   217→    try:
   218→        from tools.skills_tool import SKILLS_DIR, _parse_frontmatter, skill_matches_platform, _get_disabled_skill_names
   219→        from agent.skill_utils import get_external_skills_dirs
   220→        disabled = _get_disabled_skill_names()
   221→        seen_names: set = set()
   222→
   223→        # Scan local dir first, then external dirs
   224→        dirs_to_scan = []
   225→        if SKILLS_DIR.exists():
   226→            dirs_to_scan.append(SKILLS_DIR)
   227→        dirs_to_scan.extend(get_external_skills_dirs())
   228→
   229→        for scan_dir in dirs_to_scan:
   230→            for skill_md in scan_dir.rglob("SKILL.md"):
   231→                if any(part in ('.git', '.github', '.hub') for part in skill_md.parts):
   232→                    continue
   233→                try:
   234→                    content = skill_md.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   235→                    frontmatter, body = _parse_frontmatter(content)
   236→                    # Skip skills incompatible with the current OS platform
   237→                    if not skill_matches_platform(frontmatter):
   238→                        continue
   239→                    name = frontmatter.get('name', skill_md.parent.name)
   240→                    if name in seen_names:
   241→                        continue
   242→                    # Respect user's disabled skills config
   243→                    if name in disabled:
   244→                        continue
   245→                    description = frontmatter.get('description', '')
   246→                    if not description:
   247→                        for line in body.strip().split('\n'):
   248→                            line = line.strip()
   249→                            if line and not line.startswith('#'):
   250→                                description = line[:80]
   251→                                break
   252→                    seen_names.add(name)
   253→                    # Normalize to hyphen-separated slug, stripping
   254→                    # non-alnum chars (e.g. +, /) to avoid invalid
   255→                    # Telegram command names downstream.
   256→                    cmd_name = name.lower().replace(' ', '-').replace('_', '-')
   257→                    cmd_name = _SKILL_INVALID_CHARS.sub('', cmd_name)
   258→                    cmd_name = _SKILL_MULTI_HYPHEN.sub('-', cmd_name).strip('-')
   259→                    if not cmd_name:
   260→                        continue
   261→                    _skill_commands[f"/{cmd_name}"] = {
   262→                        "name": name,
   263→                        "description": description or f"Invoke the {name} skill",
   264→                        "skill_md_path": str(skill_md),
   265→                        "skill_dir": str(skill_md.parent),
   266→                    }
   267→                except Exception:
   268→                    continue
   269→    except Exception:
   270→        pass
   271→    return _skill_commands
   272→
   273→
   274→def get_skill_commands() -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
   275→    """Return the current skill commands mapping (scan first if empty)."""
   276→    if not _skill_commands:
   277→        scan_skill_commands()
   278→    return _skill_commands
   279→
   280→
   281→def resolve_skill_command_key(command: str) -> Optional[str]:
   282→    """Resolve a user-typed /command to its canonical skill_cmds key.
   283→
   284→    Skills are always stored with hyphens — ``scan_skill_commands`` normalizes
   285→    spaces and underscores to hyphens when building the key. Hyphens and
   286→    underscores are treated interchangeably in user input: this matches
   287→    ``_check_unavailable_skill`` and accommodates Telegram bot-command names
   288→    (which disallow hyphens, so ``/claude-code`` is registered as
   289→    ``/claude_code`` and comes back in the underscored form).
   290→
   291→    Returns the matching ``/slug`` key from ``get_skill_commands()`` or
   292→    ``None`` if no match.
   293→    """
   294→    if not command:
   295→        return None
   296→    cmd_key = f"/{command.replace('_', '-')}"
   297→    return cmd_key if cmd_key in get_skill_commands() else None
   298→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Skills Tool Module
     4→
     5→This module provides tools for listing and viewing skill documents.
     6→Skills are organized as directories containing a SKILL.md file (the main instructions)
     7→and optional supporting files like references, templates, and examples.
     8→
     9→Inspired by Anthropic's Claude Skills system with progressive disclosure architecture:
    10→- Metadata (name ≤64 chars, description ≤1024 chars) - shown in skills_list
    11→- Full Instructions - loaded via skill_view when needed
    12→- Linked Files (references, templates) - loaded on demand
    13→
    14→Directory Structure:
    15→    skills/
    16→    ├── my-skill/
    17→    │   ├── SKILL.md           # Main instructions (required)
    18→    │   ├── references/        # Supporting documentation
    19→    │   │   ├── api.md
    20→    │   │   └── examples.md
    21→    │   ├── templates/         # Templates for output
    22→    │   │   └── template.md
    23→    │   └── assets/            # Supplementary files (agentskills.io standard)
    24→    └── category/              # Category folder for organization
    25→        └── another-skill/
    26→            └── SKILL.md
    27→
    28→SKILL.md Format (YAML Frontmatter, agentskills.io compatible):
    29→    ---
    30→    name: skill-name              # Required, max 64 chars
    31→    description: Brief description # Required, max 1024 chars
    32→    version: 1.0.0                # Optional
    33→    license: MIT                  # Optional (agentskills.io)
    34→    platforms: [macos]            # Optional — restrict to specific OS platforms
    35→                                  #   Valid: macos, linux, windows
    36→                                  #   Omit to load on all platforms (default)
    37→    prerequisites:                # Optional — legacy runtime requirements
    38→      env_vars: [API_KEY]         #   Legacy env var names are normalized into
    39→                                  #   required_environment_variables on load.
    40→      commands: [curl, jq]        #   Command checks remain advisory only.
    41→    compatibility: Requires X     # Optional (agentskills.io)
    42→    metadata:                     # Optional, arbitrary key-value (agentskills.io)
    43→      hermes:
    44→        tags: [fine-tuning, llm]
    45→        related_skills: [peft, lora]
    46→    ---
    47→
    48→    # Skill Title
    49→
    50→    Full instructions and content here...
    51→
    52→Available tools:
    53→- skills_list: List skills with metadata (progressive disclosure tier 1)
    54→- skill_view: Load full skill content (progressive disclosure tier 2-3)
    55→
    56→Usage:
    57→    from tools.skills_tool import skills_list, skill_view, check_skills_requirements
    58→
    59→    # List all skills (returns metadata only - token efficient)
    60→    result = skills_list()
    61→
    62→    # View a skill's main content (loads full instructions)
    63→    content = skill_view("axolotl")
    64→
    65→    # View a reference file within a skill (loads linked file)
    66→    content = skill_view("axolotl", "references/dataset-formats.md")
    67→"""
    68→
    69→import json
    70→import logging
    71→
    72→from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home, display_hermes_home
    73→import os
    74→import re
    75→from enum import Enum
    76→from pathlib import Path
    77→from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional, Set, Tuple

    79→from tools.registry import registry, tool_error

    83→
    84→# All skills live in ~/.hermes/skills/ (seeded from bundled skills/ on install).
    85→# This is the single source of truth -- agent edits, hub installs, and bundled
    86→# skills all coexist here without polluting the git repo.
    87→HERMES_HOME = get_hermes_home()
    88→SKILLS_DIR = HERMES_HOME / "skills"

    90→# Anthropic-recommended limits for progressive disclosure efficiency

    92→MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH = 1024
    93→
    94→# Platform identifiers for the 'platforms' frontmatter field.

    96→_PLATFORM_MAP = {
    97→    "macos": "darwin",

    99→    "windows": "win32",
   100→}
   101→_ENV_VAR_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$")
   102→_EXCLUDED_SKILL_DIRS = frozenset((".git", ".github", ".hub"))

   107→def load_env() -> Dict[str, str]:
   108→    """Load profile-scoped environment variables from HERMES_HOME/.env."""
   109→    env_path = get_hermes_home() / ".env"
   110→    env_vars: Dict[str, str] = {}
   111→    if not env_path.exists():
   112→        return env_vars
   113→
   114→    with env_path.open() as f:
   115→        for line in f:
   116→            line = line.strip()
   117→            if line and not line.startswith("#") and "=" in line:
   118→                key, _, value = line.partition("=")
   119→                env_vars[key.strip()] = value.strip().strip("\"'")
   120→    return env_vars

   148→def skill_matches_platform(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   149→    """Check if a skill is compatible with the current OS platform.
   150→
   151→    Delegates to ``agent.skill_utils.skill_matches_platform`` — kept here
   152→    as a public re-export so existing callers don't need updating.
   153→    """

   155→    return _impl(frontmatter)

   158→def _normalize_prerequisite_values(value: Any) -> List[str]:
   159→    if not value:
   160→        return []
   161→    if isinstance(value, str):
   162→        value = [value]
   163→    return [str(item) for item in value if str(item).strip()]
   164→
   165→
   166→def _collect_prerequisite_values(
   167→    frontmatter: Dict[str, Any],
   168→) -> Tuple[List[str], List[str]]:
   169→    prereqs = frontmatter.get("prerequisites")
   170→    if not prereqs or not isinstance(prereqs, dict):
   171→        return [], []
   172→    return (
   173→        _normalize_prerequisite_values(prereqs.get("env_vars")),
   174→        _normalize_prerequisite_values(prereqs.get("commands")),
   175→    )

   177→
   178→def _normalize_setup_metadata(frontmatter: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   179→    setup = frontmatter.get("setup")
   180→    if not isinstance(setup, dict):
   181→        return {"help": None, "collect_secrets": []}
   182→
   183→    help_text = setup.get("help")
   184→    normalized_help = (
   185→        str(help_text).strip()
   186→        if isinstance(help_text, str) and help_text.strip()
   187→        else None
   188→    )
   189→
   190→    collect_secrets_raw = setup.get("collect_secrets")
   191→    if isinstance(collect_secrets_raw, dict):
   192→        collect_secrets_raw = [collect_secrets_raw]
   193→    if not isinstance(collect_secrets_raw, list):
   194→        collect_secrets_raw = []
   195→
   196→    collect_secrets: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   197→    for item in collect_secrets_raw:
   198→        if not isinstance(item, dict):
   199→            continue
   200→

200→
   201→        env_var = str(item.get("env_var") or "").strip()
   202→        if not env_var:
   203→            continue
   204→
   205→        prompt = str(item.get("prompt") or f"Enter value for {env_var}").strip()
   206→        provider_url = str(item.get("provider_url") or item.get("url") or "").strip()
   207→
   208→        entry: Dict[str, Any] = {
   209→            "env_var": env_var,
   210→            "prompt": prompt,
   211→            "secret": bool(item.get("secret", True)),
   212→        }
   213→        if provider_url:
   214→            entry["provider_url"] = provider_url
   215→        collect_secrets.append(entry)
   216→
   217→    return {
   218→        "help": normalized_help,
   219→        "collect_secrets": collect_secrets,
   220→    }
   221→
   222→
   223→def _get_required_environment_variables(
   224→    frontmatter: Dict[str, Any],
   225→    legacy_env_vars: List[str] | None = None,
   226→) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   227→    setup = _normalize_setup_metadata(frontmatter)
   228→    required_raw = frontmatter.get("required_environment_variables")
   229→    if isinstance(required_raw, dict):
   230→        required_raw = [required_raw]
   231→    if not isinstance(required_raw, list):
   232→        required_raw = []
   233→
   234→    required: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   235→    seen: set[str] = set()
   236→
   237→    def _append_required(entry: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   238→        env_name = str(entry.get("name") or entry.get("env_var") or "").strip()
   239→        if not env_name or env_name in seen:
   240→            return
   241→        if not _ENV_VAR_NAME_RE.match(env_name):
   242→            return
   243→
   244→        normalized: Dict[str, Any] = {
   245→            "name": env_name,
   246→            "prompt": str(entry.get("prompt") or f"Enter value for {env_name}").strip(),
   247→        }
   248→
   249→        help_text = (
   250→            entry.get("help")
   251→            or entry.get("provider_url")
   252→            or entry.get("url")
   253→            or setup.get("help")
   254→        )
   255→        if isinstance(help_text, str) and help_text.strip():
   256→            normalized["help"] = help_text.strip()
   257→
   258→        required_for = entry.get("required_for")
   259→        if isinstance(required_for, str) and required_for.strip():
   260→            normalized["required_for"] = required_for.strip()
   261→
   262→        if entry.get("optional"):
   263→            normalized["optional"] = True
   264→
   265→        seen.add(env_name)
   266→        required.append(normalized)
   267→
   268→    for item in required_raw:
   269→        if isinstance(item, str):
   270→            _append_required({"name": item})
   271→            continue
   272→        if isinstance(item, dict):
   273→            _append_required(item)
   274→
   275→    for item in setup["collect_secrets"]:
   276→        _append_required(
   277→            {
   278→                "name": item.get("env_var"),
   279→                "prompt": item.get("prompt"),
   280→                "help": item.get("provider_url") or setup.get("help"),
   281→            }
   282→        )
   283→
   284→    if legacy_env_vars is None:
   285→        legacy_env_vars, _ = _collect_prerequisite_values(frontmatter)
   286→    for env_var in legacy_env_vars:
   287→        _append_required({"name": env_var})
   288→
   289→    return required
   290→
   291→
   292→def _capture_required_environment_variables(
   293→    skill_name: str,
   294→    missing_entries: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   295→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   296→    if not missing_entries:
   297→        return {
   298→            "missing_names": [],
   299→            "setup_skipped": False,
   300→            "gateway_setup_hint": None,
   301→        }
   302→
   303→    missing_names = [entry["name"] for entry in missing_entries]
   304→    if _is_gateway_surface():
   305→        return {
   306→            "missing_names": missing_names,
   307→            "setup_skipped": False,
   308→            "gateway_setup_hint": _gateway_setup_hint(),
   309→        }
   310→
   311→    if _secret_capture_callback is None:
   312→        return {
   313→            "missing_names": missing_names,
   314→            "setup_skipped": False,
   315→            "gateway_setup_hint": None,
   316→        }
   317→
   318→    setup_skipped = False
   319→    remaining_names: List[str] = []
   320→
   321→    for entry in missing_entries:
   322→        metadata = {"skill_name": skill_name}
   323→        if entry.get("help"):
   324→            metadata["help"] = entry["help"]
   325→        if entry.get("required_for"):
   326→            metadata["required_for"] = entry["required_for"]
   327→
   328→        try:
   329→            callback_result = _secret_capture_callback(
   330→                entry["name"],
   331→                entry["prompt"],
   332→                metadata,
   333→            )
   334→        except Exception:
   335→            logger.warning(
   336→                f"Secret capture callback failed for {entry['name']}", exc_info=True
   337→            )
   338→            callback_result = {
   339→                "success": False,
   340→                "stored_as": entry["name"],
   341→                "validated": False,
   342→                "skipped": True,
   343→            }
   344→
   345→        success = isinstance(callback_result, dict) and bool(
   346→            callback_result.get("success")
   347→        )
   348→        skipped = isinstance(callback_result, dict) and bool(
   349→            callback_result.get("skipped")
   350→        )
   351→        if success and not skipped:
   352→            continue
   353→
   354→        setup_skipped = True
   355→        remaining_names.append(entry["name"])
   356→
   357→    return {
   358→        "missing_names": remaining_names,
   359→        "setup_skipped": setup_skipped,
   360→        "gateway_setup_hint": None,
   361→    }
   362→

   364→def _is_gateway_surface() -> bool:
   365→    if os.getenv("HERMES_GATEWAY_SESSION"):
   366→        return True
   367→    from gateway.session_context import get_session_env
   368→    return bool(get_session_env("HERMES_SESSION_PLATFORM"))

   375→def _is_env_var_persisted(
   376→    var_name: str, env_snapshot: Dict[str, str] | None = None
   377→) -> bool:
   378→    if env_snapshot is None:
   379→        env_snapshot = load_env()
   380→    if var_name in env_snapshot:
   381→        return bool(env_snapshot.get(var_name))
   382→    return bool(os.getenv(var_name))

   385→def _remaining_required_environment_names(
   386→    required_env_vars: List[Dict[str, Any]],
   387→    capture_result: Dict[str, Any],
   388→    *,
   389→    env_snapshot: Dict[str, str] | None = None,
   390→) -> List[str]:
   391→    missing_names = set(capture_result["missing_names"])
   392→
   393→    if env_snapshot is None:
   394→        env_snapshot = load_env()
   395→    remaining = []
   396→    for entry in required_env_vars:
   397→        name = entry["name"]
   398→        if entry.get("optional"):
   399→            continue

400→        if name in missing_names or not _is_env_var_persisted(name, env_snapshot):
   401→            remaining.append(name)
   402→    return remaining

   433→def _parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], str]:
   434→    """Parse YAML frontmatter from markdown content.
   435→
   436→    Delegates to ``agent.skill_utils.parse_frontmatter`` — kept here

   443→def _get_category_from_path(skill_path: Path) -> Optional[str]:
   444→    """
   445→    Extract category from skill path based on directory structure.
   446→
   447→    For paths like: ~/.hermes/skills/mlops/axolotl/SKILL.md -> "mlops"
   448→    Also works for external skill dirs configured via skills.external_dirs.
   449→    """
   450→    # Try the module-level SKILLS_DIR first (respects monkeypatching in tests),
   451→    # then fall back to external dirs from config.
   452→    dirs_to_check = [SKILLS_DIR]
   453→    try:
   454→        from agent.skill_utils import get_external_skills_dirs
   455→        dirs_to_check.extend(get_external_skills_dirs())
   456→    except Exception:
   457→        pass
   458→    for skills_dir in dirs_to_check:
   459→        try:
   460→            rel_path = skill_path.relative_to(skills_dir)
   461→            parts = rel_path.parts
   462→            if len(parts) >= 3:
   463→                return parts[0]
   464→        except ValueError:
   465→            continue
   466→    return None
   467→
   468→
   469→def _parse_tags(tags_value) -> List[str]:
   470→    """
   471→    Parse tags from frontmatter value.
   472→
   473→    Handles:
   474→    - Already-parsed list (from yaml.safe_load): [tag1, tag2]
   475→    - String with brackets: "[tag1, tag2]"
   476→    - Comma-separated string: "tag1, tag2"
   477→
   478→    Args:
   479→        tags_value: Raw tags value — may be a list or string
   480→
   481→    Returns:
   482→        List of tag strings
   483→    """
   484→    if not tags_value:
   485→        return []
   486→
   487→    # yaml.safe_load already returns a list for [tag1, tag2]
   488→    if isinstance(tags_value, list):
   489→        return [str(t).strip() for t in tags_value if t]
   490→
   491→    # String fallback — handle bracket-wrapped or comma-separated
   492→    tags_value = str(tags_value).strip()
   493→    if tags_value.startswith("[") and tags_value.endswith("]"):
   494→        tags_value = tags_value[1:-1]
   495→
   496→    return [t.strip().strip("\"'") for t in tags_value.split(",") if t.strip()]
   497→

   500→def _get_disabled_skill_names() -> Set[str]:
   501→    """Load disabled skill names from config.
   502→
   503→    Delegates to ``agent.skill_utils.get_disabled_skill_names`` — kept here
   504→    as a public re-export so existing callers don't need updating.
   505→    """
   506→    from agent.skill_utils import get_disabled_skill_names
   507→    return get_disabled_skill_names()

   509→
   510→def _is_skill_disabled(name: str, platform: str = None) -> bool:
   511→    """Check if a skill is disabled in config."""
   512→    import os
   513→    try:
   514→        from hermes_cli.config import load_config
   515→        config = load_config()
   516→        skills_cfg = config.get("skills", {})
   517→        resolved_platform = platform or os.getenv("HERMES_PLATFORM")
   518→        if resolved_platform:
   519→            platform_disabled = skills_cfg.get("platform_disabled", {}).get(resolved_platform)
   520→            if platform_disabled is not None:
   521→                return name in platform_disabled
   522→        return name in skills_cfg.get("disabled", [])
   523→    except Exception:
   524→        return False
   525→
   526→
   527→def _find_all_skills(*, skip_disabled: bool = False) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   528→    """Recursively find all skills in ~/.hermes/skills/ and external dirs.
   529→
   530→    Args:
   531→        skip_disabled: If True, return ALL skills regardless of disabled
   532→            state (used by ``hermes skills`` config UI). Default False
   533→            filters out disabled skills.
   534→
   535→    Returns:
   536→        List of skill metadata dicts (name, description, category).
   537→    """
   538→    from agent.skill_utils import get_external_skills_dirs
   539→
   540→    skills = []
   541→    seen_names: set = set()
   542→
   543→    # Load disabled set once (not per-skill)
   544→    disabled = set() if skip_disabled else _get_disabled_skill_names()
   545→
   546→    # Scan local dir first, then external dirs (local takes precedence)
   547→    dirs_to_scan = []
   548→    if SKILLS_DIR.exists():
   549→        dirs_to_scan.append(SKILLS_DIR)
   550→    dirs_to_scan.extend(get_external_skills_dirs())
   551→
   552→    for scan_dir in dirs_to_scan:
   553→        for skill_md in scan_dir.rglob("SKILL.md"):
   554→            if any(part in _EXCLUDED_SKILL_DIRS for part in skill_md.parts):
   555→                continue
   556→
   557→            skill_dir = skill_md.parent
   558→
   559→            try:
   560→                content = skill_md.read_text(encoding="utf-8")[:4000]
   561→                frontmatter, body = _parse_frontmatter(content)
   562→
   563→                if not skill_matches_platform(frontmatter):
   564→                    continue
   565→
   566→                name = frontmatter.get("name", skill_dir.name)[:MAX_NAME_LENGTH]
   567→                if name in seen_names:
   568→                    continue
   569→                if name in disabled:
   570→                    continue
   571→
   572→                description = frontmatter.get("description", "")
   573→                if not description:
   574→                    for line in body.strip().split("\n"):
   575→                        line = line.strip()
   576→                        if line and not line.startswith("#"):
   577→                            description = line
   578→                            break
   579→
   580→                if len(description) > MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH:
   581→                    description = description[:MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH - 3] + "..."
   582→
   583→                category = _get_category_from_path(skill_md)
   584→
   585→                seen_names.add(name)
   586→                skills.append({
   587→                    "name": name,
   588→                    "description": description,
   589→                    "category": category,
   590→                })
   591→
   592→            except (UnicodeDecodeError, PermissionError) as e:
   593→                logger.debug("Failed to read skill file %s: %s", skill_md, e)
   594→                continue
   595→            except Exception as e:
   596→                logger.debug(
   597→                    "Skipping skill at %s: failed to parse: %s", skill_md, e, exc_info=True
   598→                )
   599→                continue

647:def skills_list(category: str = None, task_id: str = None) -> str:
804:def skill_view(name: str, file_path: str = None, task_id: str = None) -> str:

647→def skills_list(category: str = None, task_id: str = None) -> str:
   648→    """
   649→    List all available skills (progressive disclosure tier 1 - minimal metadata).
   650→
   651→    Returns only name + description to minimize token usage. Use skill_view() to
   652→    load full content, tags, related files, etc.
   653→
   654→    Args:
   655→        category: Optional category filter (e.g., "mlops")
   656→        task_id: Optional task identifier used to probe the active backend
   657→
   658→    Returns:
   659→        JSON string with minimal skill info: name, description, category
   660→    """
   661→    try:
   662→        if not SKILLS_DIR.exists():
   663→            SKILLS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   664→            return json.dumps(
   665→                {
   666→                    "success": True,
   667→                    "skills": [],
   668→                    "categories": [],
   669→                    "message": f"No skills found. Skills directory created at {display_hermes_home()}/skills/",
   670→                },
   671→                ensure_ascii=False,
   672→            )
   673→
   674→        # Find all skills
   675→        all_skills = _find_all_skills()
   676→
   677→        if not all_skills:
   678→            return json.dumps(
   679→                {
   680→                    "success": True,
   681→                    "skills": [],
   682→                    "categories": [],
   683→                    "message": "No skills found in skills/ directory.",
   684→                },
   685→                ensure_ascii=False,
   686→            )
   687→
   688→        # Filter by category if specified
   689→        if category:
   690→            all_skills = [s for s in all_skills if s.get("category") == category]
   691→
   692→        # Sort by category then name
   693→        all_skills.sort(key=lambda s: (s.get("category") or "", s["name"]))
   694→
   695→        # Extract unique categories
   696→        categories = sorted(
   697→            set(s.get("category") for s in all_skills if s.get("category"))
   698→        )
   699→
   700→        return json.dumps(
   701→            {
   702→                "success": True,
   703→                "skills": all_skills,
   704→                "categories": categories,
   705→                "count": len(all_skills),
   706→                "hint": "Use skill_view(name) to see full content, tags, and linked files",
   707→            },
   708→            ensure_ascii=False,
   709→        )
   710→
   711→    except Exception as e:
   712→        return tool_error(str(e), success=False)
   713→
   714→
   715→# ── Plugin skill serving ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   716→
   717→
   718→def _serve_plugin_skill(
   719→    skill_md: Path,
   720→    namespace: str,
   721→    bare: str,
   722→) -> str:
   723→    """Read a plugin-provided skill, apply guards, return JSON."""
   724→    from hermes_cli.plugins import _get_disabled_plugins, get_plugin_manager
   725→
   726→    if namespace in _get_disabled_plugins():
   727→        return json.dumps(
   728→            {
   729→                "success": False,
   730→                "error": (
   731→                    f"Plugin '{namespace}' is disabled. "
   732→                    f"Re-enable with: hermes plugins enable {namespace}"
   733→                ),
   734→            },
   735→            ensure_ascii=False,
   736→        )
   737→
   738→    try:
   739→        content = skill_md.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   740→    except Exception as e:
   741→        return json.dumps(
   742→            {"success": False, "error": f"Failed to read skill '{namespace}:{bare}': {e}"},
   743→            ensure_ascii=False,
   744→        )
   745→
   746→    parsed_frontmatter: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   747→    try:
   748→        parsed_frontmatter, _ = _parse_frontmatter(content)
   749→    except Exception:
   750→        pass
   751→
   752→    if not skill_matches_platform(parsed_frontmatter):
   753→        return json.dumps(
   754→            {
   755→                "success": False,
   756→                "error": f"Skill '{namespace}:{bare}' is not supported on this platform.",
   757→                "readiness_status": SkillReadinessStatus.UNSUPPORTED.value,
   758→            },
   759→            ensure_ascii=False,
   760→        )
   761→
   762→    # Injection scan — log but still serve (matches local-skill behaviour)
   763→    if any(p in content.lower() for p in _INJECTION_PATTERNS):
   764→        logger.warning(
   765→            "Plugin skill '%s:%s' contains patterns that may indicate prompt injection",
   766→            namespace, bare,
   767→        )
   768→
   769→    description = str(parsed_frontmatter.get("description", ""))
   770→    if len(description) > MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH:
   771→        description = description[: MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH - 3] + "..."
   772→
   773→    # Bundle context banner — tells the agent about sibling skills
   774→    try:
   775→        siblings = [
   776→            s for s in get_plugin_manager().list_plugin_skills(namespace)
   777→            if s != bare
   778→        ]
   779→        if siblings:
   780→            sib_list = ", ".join(siblings)
   781→            banner = (
   782→                f"[Bundle context: This skill is part of the '{namespace}' plugin.\n"
   783→                f"Sibling skills: {sib_list}.\n"
   784→                f"Use qualified form to invoke siblings (e.g. {namespace}:{siblings[0]}).]\n\n"
   785→            )
   786→        else:
   787→            banner = f"[Bundle context: This skill is part of the '{namespace}' plugin.]\n\n"
   788→    except Exception:
   789→        banner = ""
   790→
   791→    return json.dumps(
   792→        {
   793→            "success": True,
   794→            "name": f"{namespace}:{bare}",
   795→            "content": f"{banner}{content}" if banner else content,
   796→            "description": description,
   797→            "linked_files": None,
   798→            "readiness_status": SkillReadinessStatus.AVAILABLE.value,
   799→        },
   800→        ensure_ascii=False,
   801→    )
   802→
   803→
   804→def skill_view(name: str, file_path: str = None, task_id: str = None) -> str:
   805→    """
   806→    View the content of a skill or a specific file within a skill directory.

804→def skill_view(name: str, file_path: str = None, task_id: str = None) -> str:
   805→    """
   806→    View the content of a skill or a specific file within a skill directory.
   807→
   808→    Args:
   809→        name: Name or path of the skill (e.g., "axolotl" or "03-fine-tuning/axolotl").
   810→            Qualified names like "plugin:skill" resolve to plugin-provided skills.
   811→        file_path: Optional path to a specific file within the skill (e.g., "references/api.md")
   812→        task_id: Optional task identifier used to probe the active backend
   813→
   814→    Returns:
   815→        JSON string with skill content or error message
   816→    """
   817→    try:
   818→        # ── Qualified name dispatch (plugin skills) ──────────────────
   819→        # Names containing ':' are routed to the plugin skill registry.
   820→        # Bare names fall through to the existing flat-tree scan below.
   821→        if ":" in name:
   822→            from agent.skill_utils import is_valid_namespace, parse_qualified_name
   823→            from hermes_cli.plugins import discover_plugins, get_plugin_manager
   824→
   825→            namespace, bare = parse_qualified_name(name)
   826→            if not is_valid_namespace(namespace):
   827→                return json.dumps(
   828→                    {
   829→                        "success": False,
   830→                        "error": (
   831→                            f"Invalid namespace '{namespace}' in '{name}'. "
   832→                            f"Namespaces must match [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+."
   833→                        ),
   834→                    },
   835→                    ensure_ascii=False,
   836→                )
   837→
   838→            discover_plugins()  # idempotent
   839→            pm = get_plugin_manager()
   840→            plugin_skill_md = pm.find_plugin_skill(name)
   841→
   842→            if plugin_skill_md is not None:
   843→                if not plugin_skill_md.exists():
   844→                    # Stale registry entry — file deleted out of band
   845→                    pm.remove_plugin_skill(name)
   846→                    return json.dumps(
   847→                        {
   848→                            "success": False,
   849→                            "error": (
   850→                                f"Skill '{name}' file no longer exists at "
   851→                                f"{plugin_skill_md}. The registry entry has "
   852→                                f"been cleaned up — try again after the "
   853→                                f"plugin is reloaded."
   854→                            ),
   855→                        },
   856→                        ensure_ascii=False,
   857→                    )
   858→                return _serve_plugin_skill(plugin_skill_md, namespace, bare)
   859→
   860→            # Plugin exists but this specific skill is missing?
   861→            available = pm.list_plugin_skills(namespace)
   862→            if available:
   863→                return json.dumps(
   864→                    {
   865→                        "success": False,
   866→                        "error": f"Skill '{bare}' not found in plugin '{namespace}'.",
   867→                        "available_skills": [f"{namespace}:{s}" for s in available],
   868→                        "hint": f"The '{namespace}' plugin provides {len(available)} skill(s).",
   869→                    },
   870→                    ensure_ascii=False,
   871→                )
   872→            # Plugin itself not found — fall through to flat-tree scan
   873→            # which will return a normal "not found" with suggestions.
   874→
   875→        from agent.skill_utils import get_external_skills_dirs
   876→
   877→        # Build list of all skill directories to search
   878→        all_dirs = []
   879→        if SKILLS_DIR.exists():
   880→            all_dirs.append(SKILLS_DIR)
   881→        all_dirs.extend(get_external_skills_dirs())
   882→
   883→        if not all_dirs:
   884→            return json.dumps(
   885→                {
   886→                    "success": False,
   887→                    "error": "Skills directory does not exist yet. It will be created on first install.",
   888→                },
   889→                ensure_ascii=False,
   890→            )
   891→
   892→        skill_dir = None
   893→        skill_md = None
   894→
   895→        # Search all dirs: local first, then external (first match wins)
   896→        for search_dir in all_dirs:
   897→            # Try direct path first (e.g., "mlops/axolotl")
   898→            direct_path = search_dir / name
   899→            if direct_path.is_dir() and (direct_path / "SKILL.md").exists():
   900→                skill_dir = direct_path
   901→                skill_md = direct_path / "SKILL.md"
   902→                break
   903→            elif direct_path.with_suffix(".md").exists():
   904→                skill_md = direct_path.with_suffix(".md")
   905→                break
   906→
   907→        # Search by directory name across all dirs
   908→        if not skill_md:
   909→            for search_dir in all_dirs:
   910→                for found_skill_md in search_dir.rglob("SKILL.md"):
   911→                    if found_skill_md.parent.name == name:
   912→                        skill_dir = found_skill_md.parent
   913→                        skill_md = found_skill_md
   914→                        break

1→---
     2→name: ascii-art
     3→description: Generate ASCII art using pyfiglet (571 fonts), cowsay, boxes, toilet, image-to-ascii, remote APIs (asciified, ascii.co.uk), and LLM fallback. No API keys required.
     4→version: 4.0.0
     5→author: 0xbyt4, Hermes Agent
     6→license: MIT
     7→dependencies: []
     8→metadata:
     9→  hermes:
    10→    tags: [ASCII, Art, Banners, Creative, Unicode, Text-Art, pyfiglet, figlet, cowsay, boxes]
    11→    related_skills: [excalidraw]
    12→
    13→---
    14→
    15→# ASCII Art Skill
    16→
    17→Multiple tools for different ASCII art needs. All tools are local CLI programs or free REST APIs — no API keys required.
    18→
    19→## Tool 1: Text Banners (pyfiglet — local)
    20→
    21→Render text as large ASCII art banners. 571 built-in fonts.
    22→
    23→### Setup
    24→
    25→```bash
    26→pip install pyfiglet --break-system-packages -q
    27→```
    28→
    29→### Usage
    30→
    31→```bash
    32→python3 -m pyfiglet "YOUR TEXT" -f slant
    33→python3 -m pyfiglet "TEXT" -f doom -w 80    # Set width
    34→python3 -m pyfiglet --list_fonts             # List all 571 fonts
    35→```
    36→
    37→### Recommended fonts
    38→
    39→| Style | Font | Best for |
    40→|-------|------|----------|
    41→| Clean & modern | `slant` | Project names, headers |
    42→| Bold & blocky | `doom` | Titles, logos |
    43→| Big & readable | `big` | Banners |
    44→| Classic banner | `banner3` | Wide displays |
    45→| Compact | `small` | Subtitles |
    46→| Cyberpunk | `cyberlarge` | Tech themes |
    47→| 3D effect | `3-d` | Splash screens |
    48→| Gothic | `gothic` | Dramatic text |
    49→
    50→### Tips
    51→
    52→- Preview 2-3 fonts and let the user pick their favorite
    53→- Short text (1-8 chars) works best with detailed fonts like `doom` or `block`
    54→- Long text works better with compact fonts like `small` or `mini`
    55→
    56→## Tool 2: Text Banners (asciified API — remote, no install)
    57→
    58→Free REST API that converts text to ASCII art. 250+ FIGlet fonts. Returns plain text directly — no parsing needed. Use this when pyfiglet is not installed or as a quick alternative.
    59→
    60→### Usage (via terminal curl)
    61→
    62→```bash
    63→# Basic text banner (default font)
    64→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/ascii?text=Hello+World"
    65→
    66→# With a specific font
    67→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/ascii?text=Hello&font=Slant"
    68→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/ascii?text=Hello&font=Doom"
    69→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/ascii?text=Hello&font=Star+Wars"
    70→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/ascii?text=Hello&font=3-D"
    71→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/ascii?text=Hello&font=Banner3"
    72→
    73→# List all available fonts (returns JSON array)
    74→curl -s "https://asciified.thelicato.io/api/v2/fonts"
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Tips
    78→
    79→- URL-encode spaces as `+` in the text parameter
    80→- The response is plain text ASCII art — no JSON wrapping, ready to display
